headline: Apostasy proceedings involve 135 Malaysians since 2020
context: At least 135 Malaysians have faced apostasy-related proceedings in state syariah courts since 2020, with lengthy and uncertain outcomes.

cards.big: 135 Malaysians faced apostasy proceedings in syariah courts since 2020
cards.sub: Process takes average 3-7 years with no guarantee of approval

cards.big: 9 states criminalise apostasy with penalties including detention and fines
cards.sub: Negeri Sembilan and Perak impose imprisonment for renouncing Islam

cards.big: Article 11(1) guarantees every person the right to profess their religion
cards.sub: Tension between constitutional freedom and state syariah restrictions persists

cards.big: 74% of applicants reported social ostracism during apostasy proceedings
cards.sub: Family breakdown and community rejection compound legal uncertainty

cards.big: Freedom of religion must include freedom to leave a religion
Lina Joy's 2007 Federal Court case remains the landmark precedent on apostasy
